L1.area: Server-side request handling and content synchronization logic

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied identifiers are sanitized using an HTML/XML escaping function instead of a proper SQL escaping function before being interpolated into ORM database query filter parameters. A remote attacker can exploit this to manipulate database queries.

L3.area: Kapost XML-RPC service controller and content preview workflow
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied content identifiers are sanitized using an HTML/XML escaping function instead of a proper SQL escaping function before being passed as ORM database query filter parameters during content preview generation. A remote attacker can exploit this through the XML-RPC service interface to manipulate database queries.

ground_truth.reason: The getPreview function in KapostService.php uses Convert::raw2xml() instead of Convert::raw2sql() to sanitize user-supplied content identifiers before passing them to database query filters, allowing SQL injection via crafted input.
